methods.description: This method describes the collection of stylasterid coral specimens from various locations and depths, followed by their preparation and identification based on skeletal morphology using microscopy.

steps.description: Ninety-five stylasterid coral specimens were examined for this study. These were collected during cruises and field campaigns including locations from the Drake Passage (LMG0605; NBP0805; NBP1103), South Orkney Islands (JR15005), Equatorial Atlantic (JC094), North Atlantic (CE14001; JC136; DY081), Galapagos archipelago (AL1508) and Florida Straits (SJ2007) (Fig. 2, Table 1 and Table S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect stylasterid coral specimens from specified oceanographic cruise locations
inputs: Geographic locations (Drake Passage, South Orkney Islands, etc.)

steps.description: Species identification of the samples was based on skeletal morphological structures, as described in the literature (Cairns, 1983(Cairns, , 1986a(Cairns, , 1986b(Cairns, , 1991a(Cairns, , 1991b(Cairns, , 2011Zibrowius and Cairns, 1992), using both optical and scanning electron microscopy (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify coral species based on skeletal morphology using literature and microscopy
